expect: PHIL-19 claims mutation is purposeful. Test: classify last 50 new tools/lessons as targeted (responding to identified gap) vs untargeted (no prior signal/frontier). If >50% are untargeted, 'mutates with purpose' is overstated. Also test fidelity: do genesis/core artifacts actually replicate unchanged?

test1_mutation_purposefulness.method: Classified L-1309 through L-1358 (50 lessons, sessions S499-S508) by reading title, Cites: header, and body for references to frontiers (F-), signals (SIG-), DOMEX lanes, DUE items, belief retests, incident responses, or explicit gap-filling tasks.
test1_mutation_purposefulness.note: Even 'untargeted' lessons mostly address real operational needs (L-1348 WSL latency, L-1349 orient performance). Pure creative exploration (L-1324, L-1325 plant biology analogies) is rare at ~4%.

test2_replication_fidelity.core_md_changes_last_20_commits: 2 substantive changes in 20 commits (S472 science-quality, S347 P14). Most commits are restore-after-plumbing-error (not content changes).
test2_replication_fidelity.genesis_md_last_substantive_change: S346 (council naming origin). Only 2 substantive edits ever; rest are restore-after-plumbing-error.
test2_replication_fidelity.note: Genesis-critical files are highly stable. CORE.md has had 2 substantive changes across ~160 sessions (S347-S508). GENESIS.md has had 2 ever. The plumbing-error restores confirm: the swarm actively repairs fidelity breakdowns.

test3_zombie_rate.note: Zombie rate dropped from 80.3% (S457) to 41.9% (S508). This is partly due to tool archival (S411: 13 tools archived) and new tool creation that is immediately referenced. Still, 42% of tools are unreferenced in the last 50 lessons or core workflow scripts.

actual: PHIL-19 'mutates with purpose' is SUPPORTED. 80% of last 50 lessons (L-1309 to L-1358) are TARGETED — created in response to identified frontiers, signals, DOMEX lanes, DUE items, belief falsification, or incident response. Only 20% emerged without prior signal, and even those mostly address operational needs. Replication fidelity is strong: CORE.md unchanged since S347 (~160 sessions), GENESIS.md since S346, version tracking consistent. Zombie rate improved from 80.3% to 41.9% but remains a weak point — mutation creates tools that aren't subsequently selected for.
diff: Expected threshold was 50% targeted for 'supported'. Actual 80% exceeds threshold significantly. The mutation:selection ratio has improved (zombie rate 80%->42%) but selection pressure on tools is still weaker than on lessons. PHIL-19's claim about fidelity is well-supported; the claim about purposeful mutation is supported for lessons but weaker for tools (42% zombie rate means tools mutate partly without purpose).
conclusion_detail: PHIL-19 is supported for the lesson layer (80% targeted mutation) and the belief/genesis layer (high replication fidelity). It is CONTESTED for the tool layer, where 42% zombie rate indicates mutation without subsequent selection. The 'adaptive parameter' (fidelity:variation ratio) is not explicitly tuned — it emerges from structural enforcement (check.sh/orient.py embedding) rather than deliberate calibration.
falsified_if: Targeted ratio drops below 50% over a 50-lesson window, or zombie rate exceeds 60% after S508
